header_summary:
Recent discussions in Bitcoin development circles have revolved around the implementation and verification of the Bitcoin protocol using Lean4, with a significant focus on formal verification to enhance protocol integrity and resolve factual disputes in design discussions. The [btc-verified project](https://github.com/ProofOfKeags/btc-verified), hosted on GitHub, applies Lean4’s mathematical rigor to verify specific claims about Bitcoin’s functionality, such as the non-malleability of the Merkle Root, ensuring each corresponds uniquely to its tree which is critical for the security of block transactions. This approach provides a more accurate understanding of Bitcoin's operational mechanics, which is crucial for future optimizations and maintaining the network's integrity.

Simultaneously, the release of Bitcoin Core versions v29.4rc1 and v30.3rc1 has been announced, with binaries available for testing from the [official site](https://bitcoincore.org/bin/bitcoin-core-29.4/test.rc1) and source codes hosted on GitHub. These versions aim to rectify any critical issues before their final release, with community feedback encouraged through the project’s issue tracker on GitHub at [Report Issues](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/issues). It highlights ongoing efforts to enhance transparency and security in the development process, ensuring robust community engagement and readiness for upcoming official releases.

Furthermore, an empirical analysis of ML-DSA-87 on a SHA-256 Proof of Work chain has revealed significant data sizing for transaction inputs, emphasizing the impact of post-quantum cryptographic measures on block space usage. The detailed findings, essential for discussions on block space budgeting and cryptographic enhancements, are available for further reading at [Empirical ML-DSA-87 data](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/empirical-ml-dsa-87-data-from-a-live-sha-256-pow-chain-relevant-to-bip-360-witness-v3-sizing-discussion). This analysis is crucial for stakeholders considering the integration of advanced cryptographic measures into their systems, reflecting ongoing research and adaptation in blockchain technology to address emerging security challenges.

recent_posts.title: btc-verified: formalizing the Bitcoin protocol
recent_posts.summary: - The Bitcoin protocol's formal verification progresses with the Lean4 project at [btc-verified](https://github.com/ProofOfKeags/btc-verified).  - Initial findings highlight the unique link between Merkle Roots and Trees, crucial for transaction integrity.  - Despite strides in theoretical accuracy, discrepancies with Bitcoin Core's practical behavior remain.

recent_posts.title: Bitcoin Core v29.4rc1 is now available
recent_posts.summary:
- Bitcoin Core version v29.4rc1 is now available for download.
- The release candidate aims to rectify issues before the final version 29.4 launch.
- Users are encouraged to report problems via GitHub's issue tracker.

recent_posts.title: Bitcoin Core v30.3rc1 is now available
recent_posts.summary:
- Bitcoin Core's version v30.3rc1 is now available for testing and download.
- Detailed preliminary notes and documentation are accessible on GitHub.
- Users encountering issues with this release candidate should report them on GitHub.

recent_posts.title: Bird of Prey 2: non-malleable Schnorr + PQ signatures
recent_posts.summary:
- ML-DSA-87 uses about 7,260 bytes per transaction input on SHA-256 chains.
- A 29MB transaction analyzed took about 1.25 hours to process with an AMD EPYC processor.
- Detailed study results on ML-DSA-87 are available at a specified online resource.

recent_posts.title: Emulating OP_RAND
recent_posts.summary:
- Bob uses cryptographic techniques involving nonces and preset calculations.
- The described method reveals potential vulnerabilities in discrete logarithm masking.
- Suggested adjustments to the cryptographic scheme could improve efficiency in ZKPs.

recent_posts.title: Btc-verified: formalizing the Bitcoin protocol
recent_posts.summary: - Formal Verification in Bitcoin development ensures precise, verifiable protocol statements.  - The project uses Lean4 and mathlib for in-depth protocol analysis without foundational rebuilds.  - Verification clarifies Merkle Tree processes and proves equivalences for integrity and alignment.

active_posts.title: Prohibit Merkle Internal Node Preimages That Encode Minimal 64-Byte Transactions
active_posts.summary:
- The proposal introduces a new Bitcoin rule for encoding specific 64-byte transactions.
- It aims to prevent SPV malleability by invalidating blocks with certain Merkle tree preimages.
- The rule targets only those transactions that could be confused with internal Merkle node images.

active_posts.title: Giving teeth to expected EC disabling: P2XX(-T)(-ML)
active_posts.summary:
- Bitcoin developers are exploring enhanced security via new output types like P2TRv2.
- Proposals include Tripwire and Miner Lockdown to disable cryptographic paths against quantum threats.
- These integrated mechanisms will help ensure timely and reliable protections in the crypto network.

active_posts.title: [BIP Proposal] Strict circular economy within the Bitcoin network
active_posts.summary: - The proposal introduces a rule to maintain Bitcoin's block subsidy with a 21 million cap.  - UTXOs older than 1,048,576 blocks will be pruned to indirectly extend subsidy epochs.  - The proposal, detailed on GitHub, invites community feedback for further refinement.

active_posts.title: [BIP Draft] Segregated Data: a prunable, script-isolated block region for data carriage
active_posts.summary:
- SegData integrates a new Bitcoin block data storage method focused on efficiency without value transfer.
- It employs mechanisms allowing for data pruning, with storage cost burdens on opt-in operators.
- Potential SegData users include indexers and timestamping services, limited by non-script-evaluable data types.

active_posts.title: State of the transaction privacy work in Bitcoin
active_posts.summary:
- Bitcoin privacy focuses on sidechains, L2s, and wallet integrations, not Layer 1.
- Cost-reducing technologies like CISA are used, but don't inherently boost privacy.
- Current Bitcoin privacy efforts align with regulatory needs and protocol stability.

active_posts.title: Public key recovery for EC leaves in P2MR (BIP-360)
active_posts.summary:
- The proposal restructures BIP-360 to reduce EC witness size and enhance quantum safety.
- It introduces a recoverable EC leaf to derive public keys from signatures, cutting witness bulk.
- This approach adjusts validation logic and secures nonce generation in P2MR structures.

today_in_history_posts.title: BIP 174 thoughts
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Matejcik raises concerns about the new Bitcoin Improvement Proposal.
- Suggested rule: reject PSBTs with conflicts instead of choosing at random.
- Recommendations include a clear strategy for handling unknown fields.

today_in_history_posts.title: proposal for Lightning Network improvement proposals
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- The proposal introduces Lightning Network Improvement Proposals (LIPs) similar to BIPs.
- Rene Pickhardt has started drafting LIP-0001, outlining the process, in a personal repo.
- A focused discussion on LIPs is planned for an upcoming event in Australia.

today_in_history_posts.title: Thoughts on scaling and consensus changes (2023)
today_in_history_posts.summary: - Bitcoin scalability involves setting up 50,000 "bitcoin banks" for handling transactions.  - Innovative solutions like Coinpools and federated sidechains are central to addressing transaction costs.  - Future Bitcoin infrastructure will focus on ensuring security, auditability, and regulatory compliance.
